passage.title: Chore Chart Test
passage.text: Our family tested two ways to remember evening chores last month. Mina used one long note on the fridge, but Joon used small chore cards. After one week, Joon finished his jobs on time because the small cards showed one job at a time. Our family marked each finished chore on a chart by the table. When Mina saw the difference, she changed to small chore cards. Two evenings later, she also finished her jobs without extra reminders. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that clear job cards can make home chores easier to follow.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Learning which reminder helps with family chores
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: What did Joon use to remember his jobs?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does chore mean in the first sentence?
choices: A home job that someone needs to do
A person who visits a club
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Mina switch to small chore cards?
choices: The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
The first tool was missing from the class
She thought they would make the jobs easier to remember
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
